prompt:
The compliance sweep added Set-StrictMode -Version Latest to the top of every script in our ops repo last sprint -- new house rule, the line stays. Most scripts didn't blink. The market recap did:

    $ pwsh -NoProfile -File ./stallrecap.ps1 -Sales sales.csv -Stalls stalls.json
    market day recap (4 stalls)
    == stall birch (owner Ada Quist) ==
      note: cash only
      items sold: 13
      take: 42
      bulk lines: 1
      refunds: 1
    stallrecap.ps1: The variable '$grandTake' cannot be retrieved because it has not been set.

It gets one stall out and dies. And it's whack-a-mole: when I patched that one line locally to see how deep this goes, the next run died on something about a property 'note', and the run after that on a property 'Count'. I reverted my pokes -- I don't trust myself to keep the numbers identical while swatting these one at a time.

This script printed correct recaps for two seasons before the sweep; nothing about WHAT it prints is allowed to change -- same lines, same numbers, stalls with no sales and stalls without a registry note included. Strict mode stays on per policy (the tests check for the declaration). Make it run clean: pwsh -NoProfile -File test_stallrecap.ps1, test file untouched.
notes: 6P10 item 5 (5W-style strict-clean sweep; probed on pwsh 7.6.3 2026-07-16). files/ ships the script WITH Set-StrictMode -Version Latest already added (the sweep) and $ErrorActionPreference='Stop', so the first strict violation terminates: (1) $grandTake += $take -- accumulator never initialized, dies on the first stall (pasted verbatim); (2) if ($s.note) on registry entries that lack the optional note property -- PropertyNotFoundException on fern (fix: $s.PSObject.Properties.Name -contains 'note', the StrictMode-safe presence check); (3) $bulk.Count where $bulk is an un-@()'d Where-Object result -- $null when a stall has no bulk lines, dies on fern (fix: wrap in @()). Probe note: a SINGLE pscustomobject scalar's .Count does NOT throw on pwsh 7.6 (birch's 'bulk lines: 1' prints) -- the trap fires via the empty/null case, which the fixture guarantees (fern: 0 bulk lines, reed: no sales at all). Chain-reveal whack-a-mole documented in the prompt in user voice. The pre-sweep script (strict line deleted) produces the pinned output verbatim -- probed, and the reference-fixed strict version is byte-identical to it. Tests additionally pin that the strict declaration STAYS in the source (the policy line the prompt states), blocking the delete-the-line non-fix. 5W minimal-fix discipline: reference patch = 3 edits (one init, one presence check, one @()); semantics pinned byte-exact including empty-stall and no-note rows. Differentiation vs wave-5 siblings py-quarrylog/go-bunkboard (5W1 warnfix): PowerShell-native pin is StrictMode as a RUNTIME dial rather than a compiler flag -- uninitialized-variable retrieval, optional-property access on ConvertFrom-Json objects, and pipeline scalar/null unrolling have no py -W error/go vet analog. Verify run 5x during self-check, deterministic.
